action.reasoning: Observation: The ensemble eval in iteration 11 scored 1054.6, which is worse than the best prior single-model score of 1018.6 from iteration 5. The original best checkpoint was overwritten, so we must retrain. We have ~53 minutes remaining. Analysis: The iteration-5 config (ResNet hidden=256, 12 blocks, no residual, no channel weighting, 5 epochs) produced the best result. To beat it, we should train the same architecture longer (10–20 epochs) with a larger batch size (128) to cut epoch time, while keeping strong regularization (dropout=0.1, light weight decay). Before a long run, we need a 1-epoch measurement to confirm VRAM fits and gauge speed. Decision: Write a clean train.py that omits residual/channel-weighting, run a single epoch with batch_size=128, then scale to a full run.
